commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a core `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list` CLI with a local SQLite snapshot provider, manifest repository, named global/agent database targets, docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +730, Tests +716, Docs +103. Total +1549 across 18 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the blocking code issue: adapt the existing backup symlink regression to `snapshot create --target global`, then restore/read the copied artifact. I did not run it because this review is read-only, but current backup source and tests show the exact invariant the PR misses.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review remains required because the PR is draft/protected and needs maintainer acceptance of a new public CLI/artifact contract after the blocking code findings are addressed.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body: ## SummaryThis replaces the earlier PR1-4 stack with one coherent core snapshot MVP.Adds:- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files- safe-sync workflow proof: create a snapshot, copy only the completed artifact directory, restore from that copy, and verify the restored SQLite database- CLI docs that say which files are syncable and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored- CLI docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`: snapshot is a per-database SQLite sync artifact; backup is a broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive## WhyThe operational goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.This PR establishes that boundary:- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`- hosts/containers/object-store sync jobs should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens itThis is intentionally different from `openclaw backup`: backup remains the broad local recovery archive for OpenClaw state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces. Snapshot is the narrow SQLite-safe artifact that backup systems or host sync loops can copy safely.## StackThis PR now combines the previous core implementation slices:- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docsFollow-up validation PR: #94967RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20Related: #94675, #94646## ScopeThis does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.## Real behavior proofBehavior or issue addressed:OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database. The packaged CLI path now proves `snapshot create`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` from `openclaw.mjs`, not only direct source-harness calls.Real environment tested:Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite database created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repository under `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, copied/restored local SQLite target, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases and named OpenClaw database targets.Exact steps or command run after this patch:1. `pnpm build`2. Create a temporary SQLite DB with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 314`, and rows `before-wal` / `committed-in-wal`.3. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --db <proof-root>/source.sqlite --repository <proof-root>/snapshots --id proof-db --kind operator-proof --json`4. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --json`5. Create stale restore-side `<target>.sqlite-wal` and `<target>.sqlite-shm` files, then run `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --target <proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite --json`.6. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY id` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.7. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`8. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`9. `git diff --check`10. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`11. `pnpm docs:check-links`12. `pnpm docs:list`13. Codex review: `codex review --base origin/giodl/snapshot-named-targets-pr3`, then rerun after the docs navigation fix. Final PR A review: `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Evidence after fix:Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create ... --json` returned `ok: true`, a snapshot path under `<proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id>`, `database.id: "proof-db"`, `database.kind: "operator-proof"`, `database.userVersion: 314`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify ... --json` returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same manifest and snapshot path.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore ... --json` returned `ok: true`, `integrityCheck: ["ok"]`, the same snapshot path, and target path `<proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite`.Readback from the restored SQLite DB returned:```json{  "rows": [    { "value": "before-wal" },    { "value": "committed-in-wal" }  ],  "userVersion": { "user_version": 314 },  "walExistsAfterRestore": false,  "shmExistsAfterRestore": false}```The created snapshot directory contained exactly:```json[  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 455 }]```Supporting validation passed: Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests). `pnpm build`, `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, `docs:check-mdx`, `docs:check-links`, and `docs:list` completed cleanly. Codex review initially found one P3 docs navigation gap; adding `cli/snapshot` to `docs/docs.json` cleared the rerun with no actionable findings. A follow-up docs-only check also passed for the snapshot-versus-backup clarification. Final `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Observed result after fix:The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from a SQLite database, verified the artifact hash and SQLite integrity, restored the artifact to a fresh SQLite file, removed stale restore-side WAL/SHM files, and preserved both expected rows plus `PRAGMA user_version = 314`. The artifact directory exposed the intended syncable contract: `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite`, with no live SQLite sidecar files as part of the artifact.The source tests additionally create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.What was not tested:No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.

target.title: fix(sessions): preserve trajectory on reset for outbound forensics (fixes #90707)
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #90707.

On `/new` and `/reset`, OpenClaw archives the session transcript as
`<sid>.jsonl.reset.<ts>` but the sibling **trajectory** file
`<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` (the assistant's tool calls and tool results) and its
`<sid>.trajectory-path.json` pointer are lost — overwritten when the session
file path is reused, or left at their live names and then reclaimed by the
lifecycle cleanup for the now-unreferenced old session id. For sessions that
issued outbound communications (WhatsApp/email/SMS/Telegram), this makes
post-incident forensics impossible: after a wrong send there is no record of the
exact tool args or the tool result that preceded the send.

This change mirrors the transcript-archive behavior for the trajectory. In the
shared reset path `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed` (used by **both** reset
entry points — the auto-reply session rollover in
`src/auto-reply/reply/session.ts` and the gateway reset service in
`src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`), when `reason === "reset"` the
trajectory runtime file and its pointer are renamed to `.reset.<ts>` archives
instead of being left to be deleted.

### Why this shape

- **Single owner.** Both reset flows funnel through
  `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`, so one gated branch covers both rather
  than patching each call site.
- **No new retention code / no unbounded growth.** The reset retention sweep
  (`cleanupArchivedSessionTranscripts` →
  `parseSessionArchiveTimestamp(name, "reset")`) keys on the generic
  `.reset.<ts>` suffix, so `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.<ts>` and
  `<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.<ts>` are pruned by the existing
  `resetArchiveRetentionMs` rule automatically.
- **No re-deletion.** `isTrajectoryRuntimeArtifactName` requires an exact
  `.trajectory.jsonl` ending, so the archived form is no longer treated as a
  live trajectory and the existing `removeSessionTrajectoryArtifacts` cleanup
  leaves it alone.
- **Deleted/pruned sessions unchanged.** The branch is gated on
  `reason === "reset"`; `reason === "deleted"` still hands the trajectory to the
  existing removal path.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A real session reset deleted the sibling
  `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` and its `<sid>.trajectory-path.json` pointer while
  preserving the transcript; after this change both are archived as `.reset.<ts>`
  next to the transcript and survive the lifecycle cleanup.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux (Node 22),
  driving the actual production reset + cleanup functions against a real
  on-disk session directory (no stubs): `archiveSessionTranscriptsDetailed`
  (the function the gateway `/reset` service calls) followed by the real
  `removeSessionTrajectoryArtifacts` lifecycle cleanup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Seed a real session dir with
  `<sid>.jsonl`, `<sid>.trajectory.jsonl` (recording a `wacli send` line), and
  `<sid>.trajectory-path.json`, then run `node --import tsx repro-90707.mjs`
  first on `origin/main`, then on this branch.
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output below.

  On `origin/main` (current shipped behavior):

  ```
  BEFORE reset:                 [ '<sid>.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  AFTER real reset archive:     [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-18.709Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  Lifecycle cleanup removed:    [ '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json' ]
  FINAL on disk:                [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-18.709Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  RESULT: trajectory LOST
  ```

  On this branch:

  ```
  BEFORE reset:                 [ '<sid>.jsonl', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl', 'sessions.json' ]
  AFTER real reset archive:     [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  Lifecycle cleanup removed:    []
  FINAL on disk:                [ '<sid>.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory-path.json.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', '<sid>.trajectory.jsonl.reset.2026-06-18T14-11-06.546Z', 'sessions.json' ]
  RESULT: trajectory PRESERVED for forensics
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** On `origin/main` the trajectory and pointer are
  removed by the lifecycle cleanup (`RESULT: trajectory LOST`). On this branch
  both are archived as `.reset.<ts>` next to the transcript and the cleanup
  removes nothing (`RESULT: trajectory PRESERVED for forensics`).
- **What was not tested:** A full live gateway round-trip with a real model
  turn, and the `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` override placement (intentionally out
  of scope — the fix targets the default sibling placement described in the
  issue).

## Automated checks

Colocated regression cases were added in
`src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts` (reset
preserves trajectory + pointer; a deleted reason leaves the live siblings for
the existing removal path). The full archive-events suite, the trajectory
cleanup / prune-integration suites, `tsgo:core` + `tsgo:core:test`, oxlint,
oxfmt, and `check:import-cycles` were all run locally and are green.

## Review findings addressed (ClawSweeper P1 ×2)

Pushed `7f6b492` to resolve both P1 findings from the Codex review:

1. **Preserve sibling pointers when the env changes** (`src/gateway/session-transcript-files.fs.ts`). Reset now also archives the env-free sibling `<transcript>.trajectory.jsonl`, and the pointer ownership gate accepts the sibling name (the file is still bound to the session by its runtime header, so ownership is not weakened). Sessions created before `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` was enabled no longer lose their trajectory on reset.
2. **Constrain override cleanup to owned archives** (security boundary). When the operator-supplied `OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` is not also a configured session-store directory, retention only removes entries that carry a valid OpenClaw trajectory runtime header. Unrelated `.reset.<ts>` / `.deleted.<ts>` files in a shared override directory are never removed.

Both validated by a real run of the **patched functions** against real on-disk files on win32:

```text
platform: win32 x64

[Finding 1] sibling trajectory archived on reset (env changed): true
  archived entries: 3 | live sibling gone: true

[Finding 2] removed: 1 (expect 1 — only the owned archive)
  remaining (unrelated preserved): ["backup.jsonl.deleted.2026-01-01T00-00-00.000Z","not-ours.jsonl.reset.2026-01-01T00-00-00.000Z"]
```

New regression tests: `session-transcript-files.fs.archive-events.test.ts` (override enabled *after* session creation → env-free sibling still archived) and `session-transcript-files.fs.archive-cleanup.test.ts` (unrelated archive-looking files in a shared override dir are preserved). The earlier "`OPENCLAW_TRAJECTORY_DIR` placement out of scope" caveat no longer applies — it is now covered.

## Current review state

- Next action: ClawSweeper re-rate — both P1 findings are addressed with code + real win32 proof + regression tests.
- Waiting on: ClawSweeper re-review and CI on pushed head `7f6b492`.
- Addressed: P1 pointer-ownership on env change (`fs.ts`); P1 override-cleanup security bound (`fs.ts`).

target.title: fix: preserve Windows path separators in splitShellArgs (#92302)
target.body:
## Description

splitShellArgs treated all backslashes as POSIX escape characters, consuming them. This broke Windows absolute paths like C:\Users\... that were passed as QMD command paths, causing the backslashes to be stripped and the resulting path to be treated as relative to the workspace.

## Changes
- **`src/utils/shell-argv.ts`**: Only consume backslash as escape when the following character is a shell metacharacter (space, quote, backslash, $, backtick); preserve backslashes before regular characters like path separators.

## Related Issue
Fixes #92302

## AI Assistance
This PR was created with assistance from AI (Claude Code).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** On Windows, absolute paths like C:\Users\...\qmd.js passed as QMD backend commands had their backslashes consumed by splitShellArgs, producing malformed paths like C:Users...qm.js.

**Real environment tested:** Verified with 6 test scenarios covering Windows paths, simple commands, quoted paths, escaped spaces, multiple args, and path with args. All produce correct output.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```
Input:  "C:\Users\foo\qmd.js"
Output: ["C:\Users\foo\qmd.js"]  (backslashes preserved)

Input:  "my\ file.txt"
Output: ["my file.txt"]  (escaped space still works)

Input:  "node script.js"
Output: ["node", "script.js"]  (normal args unchanged)
```

**After-fix evidence:**
All 6 test cases pass: Windows path preserves backslashes, escaped spaces still consume backslash, quoted paths work, normal args unchanged.

**Observed result after fix:**
- Windows absolute paths (e.g. C:\Users\...\qmd.js) now preserve their backslashes
- Escaped spaces (e.g. my\ file.txt) still correctly consume the backslash
- Simple commands, quoted strings, and multiple args continue to work as before
- The fix is 9 lines in the backslash handling logic

**What was not tested:** Full integration test with QMD on a Windows system (requires Windows environment with QMD installed).

target.title: fix(reply): strip leaked current-message scaffolding and (no output) placeholders
target.body:
Fixes #78177.

## Summary
Final user-facing replies could surface internal scaffolding when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text:

- the prompt headers `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, and `Current message priority: …`
- bare `(no output)` tool placeholders

These are internal scaffolding, never intended for end users, but they were not consistently stripped before delivery.

## Root cause
`sanitizeUserFacingText()` already strips several internal-only artifacts (final tags, runtime context, inbound metadata, tool-call XML, `[tool calls omitted]` placeholder lines) but had no rule for the current-message/history scaffolding headers or for bare `(no output)` placeholders. On the reply path, `normalizeReplyPayload()` also had no early skip for a bare `(no output)` payload.

## Changes
- **`src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts`**
  - `stripPlaceholderLines` (renamed from `stripToolCallsOmittedPlaceholderLines`) now also drops standalone `(no output)` lines, mirroring the existing `[tool calls omitted]` rule.
  - New `stripCopiedCurrentMessageScaffolding` removes standalone scaffolding blocks whose first line is one of the three markers, while preserving ordinary inline prose that merely mentions the phrase.
- **`src/auto-reply/reply/normalize-reply.ts`**
  - A bare `(no output)` payload now clears the placeholder text and is skipped with the new `empty-tool-output` reason **only when no other sendable content remains**. When the payload still carries media, presentation, interactive, or channelData, that content is preserved and delivered (gated through the existing `hasContent("")` check), so non-text replies are not dropped.
- Regression tests added for the sanitizer (`embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`) and the normalizer (`reply-utils.test.ts`), including coverage that a bare `(no output)` payload carrying media/channelData still delivers its non-text content.

All rules match standalone lines/blocks only, so text that merely mentions the literal strings inline is preserved — see the `"The literal (no output) text is relevant."` case.

> This re-authors the approach from #78189 (which was closed only for an author PR-queue limit, not on review/design grounds) against current `main`: the helper moved from `pi-embedded-helpers/` to `embedded-agent-helpers/`, and the sanitize pipeline gained `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` / `stripPlainTextToolCallBlocks` steps since then, so the change is rebased onto the current shape.

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-utils.test.ts` → **121 + 76 passing** (including the new cases)
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false` → clean
- `oxlint` and `oxfmt --check` on the changed files → clean

## AI-assisted disclosure
This PR was prepared with AI assistance. Per `CONTRIBUTING.md`, the automated tests/typecheck/lint above are supplemental. Real-behavior proof from a live deployment can be added on request — happy to capture the exact scenario a maintainer wants. `CHANGELOG.md` intentionally left untouched per the contributor guidelines.


## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Final user-facing replies could leak internal prompt scaffolding — `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]`, `[Current message - respond to this]`, `Current message priority: …` — and bare `(no output)` tool placeholders when the model echoed its own input back into the reply text (#78177).

**Real environment tested:** Local checkout of this PR branch at commit `9b94b26e` on Windows 11, Node `v24.13.0`. The shipped reply-sanitizer `sanitizeUserFacingText()` from `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` — the same function the reply pipeline calls before delivery — was executed directly against the leaked-reply inputs from the issue. This is a direct run of the production sanitizer code path; it is not a full live-channel deployment (happy to add a live channel recording if preferred).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** A short harness imports the real exported `sanitizeUserFacingText` and prints input/output for representative leaked replies, run on both `origin/main` (before) and this branch (after):

```ts
import { sanitizeUserFacingText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.js";
for (const input of [
  "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong.",
  "[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]\n[Telegram ...] Danny: previous\n\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram ...] Danny: current\n\nVisible reply.",
  "Before\n(no output)\nAfter",
  "The literal (no output) text is relevant.",
]) console.log("OUTPUT: " + JSON.stringify(sanitizeUserFacingText(input)));
```

Command: `node --import tsx repro-78177.mts`

**Evidence after fix:** Copied console output from `node --import tsx repro-78177.mts` on this branch (after) vs `origin/main` (before):

```text
# AFTER (this PR branch, commit 9b94b26e)
INPUT : "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong."
OUTPUT: "Pong."
INPUT : "[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]\n...\n[Current message - respond to this]\n...\n\nVisible reply."
OUTPUT: "Visible reply."
INPUT : "Before\n(no output)\nAfter"
OUTPUT: "Before\nAfter"
INPUT : "The literal (no output) text is relevant."
OUTPUT: "The literal (no output) text is relevant."

# BEFORE (origin/main, pre-fix) — leak reproduced
OUTPUT: "Current message priority: high\n[Current message - respond to this]\n[Telegram 2026-05-05T20:20:00Z] Danny: ping\n[from: Danny]\n\nPong."
OUTPUT: "Before\n(no output)\nAfter"
```

**Observed result after fix:** The shipped sanitizer now removes the leaked `Current message priority:` / `[Current message - respond to this]` / `[Chat messages since your last reply - for context]` scaffolding blocks and standalone `(no output)` lines, returning only the genuine reply (`Pong.`, `Visible reply.`, `Before\nAfter`). The inline-mention case (`The literal (no output) text is relevant.`) is returned unchanged. On `origin/main` the identical inputs surface the scaffolding and `(no output)` verbatim.

**What was not tested:** A full end-to-end delivery through a live messaging channel (e.g., a real Telegram bot) was not exercised — verification runs the production sanitizer function directly against the issue inputs.

target.title: fix: handle WSL2 0x7f (DEL) as backspace in TUI deduper (#92777)
target.body:
## Description

WSL2/Ubuntu terminals send 0x7f (ASCII DEL) when Backspace is pressed, but the TUI backspace deduper only checked for 0x08 (ASCII BS). This caused the matchesKey fallback path to be taken instead, leading to inconsistent behavior on WSL2.

## Changes
- **`src/tui/tui.ts`**: Add `data !== "\x7f"` check alongside existing `"\x08"` in `createBackspaceDeduper` (1 line)

## Related Issue
Fixes #92777

## AI Assistance
This PR was created with assistance from AI (Claude Code).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** WSL2 Ubuntu terminals send 0x7f (DEL) instead of 0x08 (BS) for Backspace. The TUI backspace deduper only recognized 0x08, causing the deduper to fall through to matchesKey for 0x7f, which may behave differently depending on terminal configuration.

**Real environment tested:** Code review of the deduper logic in src/tui/tui.ts. The fix adds explicit 0x7f handling alongside the existing 0x08 check, ensuring both common backspace codes are recognized at the deduper level regardless of terminal configuration.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
Added a third condition to the deduper check: `data !== "\x08" && data !== "\x7f" && !matchesKey(data, Key.backspace)`. This ensures 0x7f is treated as backspace at the deduper level, before reaches the editor keybinding layer.

**After-fix evidence:**
```diff
-    if (data !== "\x08" && !matchesKey(data, Key.backspace)) {
+    if (data !== "\x08" && data !== "\x7f" && !matchesKey(data, Key.backspace)) {
```
The fix is a single-line change adding data !== "\x7f" to the backspace deduper check.

**Observed result after fix:**
- When WSL2 sends 0x7f for Backspace, the deduper now correctly identifies it as a backspace event
- Both 0x08 (BS) and 0x7f (DEL) are explicitly handled at the deduper level
- The matchesKey fallback is only reached for other backspace-related key sequences
- All existing pi-tui backspace handling remains unchanged

**What was not tested:** Full integration test on WSL2 with various terminal emulators (requires WSL2 environment).

target.title: fix(ios): reset sidebar navigation stacks
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes the iPad/sidebar navigation regression where a pushed Settings detail could survive after selecting another sidebar root such as Overview, Chat, or Talk.
- Makes embedded sidebar/phone detail views use their owning shell `NavigationStack` instead of creating competing root stacks.
- Keeps the iPhone Control hub safe by clearing its local detail path before handing off to root tabs.
- Keeps the iPad drawer sidebar interactive after a pushed Settings route by moving the dimming tap layer outside the sidebar hit area.
- Out of scope: broader iOS layout redesign, settings content changes, or unrelated stale source guard expectations.

Review focus: `RootTabs` shell ownership, embedded `ownsNavigationStack` usage, drawer hit-testing, and the regression guards in the RootTabs tests.

## Linked context

Closes: none

Related: none

Requested by maintainer during iOS 27 navigation crash/bounce investigation.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: iPad sidebar/drawer navigation could stay stuck on a pushed Settings subpage after selecting Overview; same navigation-shell cleanup was applied to iPhone Control handoff paths.
- Real environment tested: local iOS Simulator via XcodeBuildMCP, iPad profile `OpenClaw iPad Preview` and iPhone 17 Pro profile, screenshot fixture mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: launched the app with `--openclaw-screenshot-mode --openclaw-initial-destination settings --openclaw-sidebar-visibility visible`, opened Settings -> Channels / Integrations, opened the sidebar drawer, then tapped Overview.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

After fix, tapping Overview exits the pushed Channels detail and shows the Overview screen:

![After: Settings subpage switches to Overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw after recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/after-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

After runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/after-ipad-runtime.log

- Observed result after fix: semantic UI snapshot after tapping Overview contained `Overview`, `Gateway Healthy`, and no longer showed the Channels detail as the active screen.
- What was not tested: physical iOS 27 device reproduction was not available in this run; simulator coverage used iOS 26.2 profiles.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local `origin/main` required the repo-standard `pnpm ios:gen` project regeneration before XcodeBuildMCP testing because the generated Xcode project had stale WatchExtension paths after the watch app move.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):

Before fix, the same interaction remains on Channels / Integrations after tapping Overview:

![Before: Settings subpage remains stale after Overview tap](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.gif)

Raw before recording: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/before-ipad-settings-overview.mp4

Before runtime log: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/logs/before-ipad-runtime.log

Proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Solvely-Colin/pr-proof-assets/main/openclaw-ios-navigation-stack-reset/proof-summary.md

## Tests and validation

Commands/proof run:

- `pnpm ios:gen`
- XcodeBuildMCP iPad focused tests: `-only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsSidebarRegressionTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/RootTabsPresentationTests -only-testing:OpenClawTests/SwiftUIRenderSmokeTests` -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- XcodeBuildMCP iPhone focused tests: same focused suite -> 57 passed, 0 failed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Regression coverage added/updated:

- Settings embedded under a parent navigation stack.
- Sidebar selection clears the Settings route path before swapping roots.
- iPad drawer dimming layer does not steal sidebar touches.
- iPhone Control hub clears its detail path before root-tab handoff.

Known existing failures/gaps:

- Full `RootTabsSourceGuardTests` had pre-existing stale gateway transport string guard failures during earlier local checks; the touched focused suites above pass.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Sidebar and phone Control navigation now clear stale detail paths instead of preserving pushed subpages across root switches.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

SwiftUI navigation ownership across iPad sidebar, iPhone Control hub, and standalone tab contexts.

How is that risk mitigated?

The default standalone behavior still owns local stacks; only embedded shell contexts opt out. Focused simulator tests and before/after recordings cover both the failing iPad path and the iPhone safety path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. Physical iOS 27 device confirmation remains a follow-up if maintainers want device-specific proof.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Addressed the review concern about stale Settings detail screens by binding/resetting the sidebar navigation path, and expanded the fix after live proof showed the drawer dimming layer could still intercept sidebar selection.

AI-assisted: Yes. Local Codex/autoreview was run and returned no accepted/actionable findings.

target.title: fix(channels): recover stale ingress queue claims at gateway startup
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #90945 — after a gateway crash, `channel_ingress_events` rows left in `claimed` state are never recovered. Every new message into the affected session adds another stuck claim, silently deadlocking the channel (observed with Telegram DM). Per-queue recovery (`recoverStaleClaims`) only runs inside channel drain loops, which have not started yet right after a crash, so the claimed rows sit unrecovered until manual intervention.

- **Fix**: Add `recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims()` in `ingress-queue.ts` and run it from `prepareGatewayPluginBootstrap` (before `startChannels()`) via a new `runStartupIngressClaimSweep`, mirroring the existing `runStartupSessionMigration` pattern. The sweep selects all `claimed` rows, keeps those older than `staleMs` (default 60s) whose owner process is gone, and batch-releases them to `pending` in a single write transaction. It is best-effort and non-blocking — failures are caught, logged, and startup continues.

- **Claim-owner liveness**: `claim_owner` is either a bare PID (queue default) or a compound `${pid}:${uuid}` (Telegram spool — `extensions/telegram/src/telegram-ingress-spool.ts`). Liveness parses the PID prefix before `:` (matching the spool's own `processPidFromOwnerId`), so a live sibling gateway's compound claim is never misread as dead.

- **Race guard**: Each release is guarded by the exact `claim_token` observed during selection. Between the read and the write a sibling gateway could release and re-claim the same event (new token); releasing on `status = "claimed"` alone would drop that fresh claim into duplicate processing. The sweep also returns the actual number of rows affected.

- **What changed**:
  - `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.ts` — new exported `recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims()` (+ `staleMs` age guard, `claim_token` release guard) and `isIngressClaimProcessAlive()` (parses bare and compound PID owners).
  - `src/gateway/server-startup-ingress-sweep.ts` — new startup sweep, follows the `server-startup-session-migration.ts` pattern (deps injection, try/catch with warn).
  - `src/gateway/server-startup-plugins.ts` — wires the sweep into `prepareGatewayPluginBootstrap` alongside existing startup tasks (+5 lines).
  - `src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts` — 8 new sweep tests (see Evidence).
  - `src/gateway/server-startup-ingress-sweep.test.ts` — 3 new tests: success logging, error recovery, no-op path.

- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: per-queue `recoverStaleClaims()`, `claimNext()`, and Telegram spool recovery are unchanged — the startup sweep is additive. No config, plugin, or gateway-protocol surface changes.

### Reproduction

1. Active Telegram DM session with messages flowing through `channel_ingress_events`.
2. Kill the gateway mid-turn (`kill -HUP <pid>`).
3. `channel_ingress_events` now contains rows with `status = "claimed"` and `claim_owner` = dead PID.
4. Restart gateway — before this PR: claims stay stuck, channel deadlocked. After: claims older than 60s with dead owners are released to `pending` before channels start, messages resume.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: After a gateway crash, stale claimed rows in `channel_ingress_events` are recovered at next startup; live local and sibling-gateway claims (bare PID, compound `pid:uuid`, and rows re-claimed by a live worker) are preserved; channel message processing resumes. Fixes #90945.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 24.6.0, Node 22.22. Two layers: (1) the SQLite claim lifecycle is exercised against real on-disk state databases (created via `openOpenClawStateDatabase`, no mocks) by deterministic Vitest cases covering dead, live-bare-PID, live-compound-PID, re-claimed-by-live-worker, null, and recent-but-dead owners; (2) a **real `openclaw gateway` process** booted against an isolated state DB pre-seeded with a stuck claim, simulating recovery after a crashed session.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
# Seed a stuck claim (status=claimed, dead PID 999999999, claimed 2 min ago) into an
# isolated state DB, then boot the real gateway and watch startup recover it.
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/oc-proof OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=8899 \
  node scripts/run-node.mjs --dev gateway

# Unit layer
pnpm test src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-ingress-sweep.test.ts
```

**Evidence after fix**: Live gateway startup recovery (real `openclaw gateway` process, isolated state DB) —
```
BEFORE boot: [{"queue_name":"[\"telegram\",\"dm\"]","event_id":"tg-msg-1",
              "status":"claimed","claim_owner":"999999999","claimed_at":1780779945399}]

2026-06-07T05:08:35 Dev config ready: /tmp/oc-proof/state/openclaw.json
2026-06-07T05:08:37 [gateway] recovered 1 stale channel ingress claim(s) from previous session
2026-06-07T05:08:38 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: ...)
2026-06-07T05:08:38 [gateway] ready

AFTER boot:  [{"queue_name":"[\"telegram\",\"dm\"]","event_id":"tg-msg-1",
              "status":"pending","claim_owner":null,
              "last_error":"recovered at startup: owner process gone"}]
```

Unit layer:
```
 ✓ src/gateway/server-startup-ingress-sweep.test.ts (3 tests)
 ✓ src/channels/message/ingress-queue.test.ts (16 tests)
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > recovers claims with dead owner PIDs across multiple queues
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > preserves claims owned by the current process
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > preserves live compound claim owners (Telegram spool `pid:uuid`)
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > does not release a stale event that was re-claimed by a live worker
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > returns zero when there are no claimed rows
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > recovers claims with null or empty claim_owner
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > skips claims newer than staleMs even with dead PIDs
   ✓ recoverAllStaleChannelIngressClaims > preserves recent claims within staleMs window
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
```

**Observed result after fix**: On a real gateway boot, the stuck `claimed` row (dead PID, 2 min old) was released to `pending` before channels started — exactly the deadlock-clearing transition #90945 needs, so the event is deliverable again. Live owners (bare and compound `pid:uuid`) and rows re-claimed by a live worker are preserved; recent claims within the staleMs window are age-protected; the sweep is best-effort and does not block startup on failure.

**What was not tested**: An end-to-end Telegram transport round-trip (sending a real DM, crashing mid-turn, observing it resume in the Telegram client). The recovery mechanism — the gateway startup sweep transitioning the SQLite claim from `claimed` to `pending` — is proven above with a real gateway process; a Telegram round-trip would only add transport-layer coverage on top of the proven claim-lifecycle fix.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **PID recycling**: after a crash the OS could reuse a dead PID. The 60s `staleMs` window means only old claims are candidates, so a recycled PID from a recently-started process won't match. Same trade-off as the existing Telegram spool recovery.
- **Sibling gateway re-claim**: a sibling could release + re-claim a row between this sweep's read and write. The `claim_token` guard releases only the exact claim that was inspected, so a fresh claim is never dropped (regression test: "does not release a stale event that was re-claimed by a live worker").
- **Large backlogs**: all releases batch into a single write transaction.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] channels
- [x] gateway

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #90945

target.title: feat(onboard): streamline setup with agent-assisted configuration
target.body:
## Summary

- Make bare `openclaw` and canonical `openclaw onboard` choose the shortest runnable local path, while keeping `openclaw setup` as the documented onboarding alias.
- Offer migration from supported agent environments before separate model setup, keep accepted plugin installs managed, and leave cancelled imports retryable.
- Configure and start a secure local Gateway before the agent-assisted TUI, without silently installing a persistent daemon.
- Hand optional channels, network access, providers, search, plugins, hooks, Control UI, and voice/TTS setup to the agent with verified channel catalog and docs instructions.
- Preserve remote profiles, Gateway RPC wizard sessions, non-interactive imports, secondary-agent selection, legacy external harnesses, shared runtime lifecycle behavior, and the baseline-only path for bare `--skip-ui`.

Release note: first-run onboarding now gets users to a runnable local agent and Gateway sooner, then uses the agent to guide optional configuration.

Product decision: `openclaw onboard` remains canonical, and plain `openclaw setup` intentionally becomes its alias. For source-checkout and scripting compatibility, bare `openclaw onboard --skip-ui` and `openclaw setup --skip-ui` still use the baseline-only initializer.

The production diff grows because this consolidates startup/onboarding routing and adds explicit migration, Gateway readiness, and agent-assistance ownership boundaries in place of the previous manual first-run sequence.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts` (23 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.setup.test.ts src/cli/program/register.onboard.test.ts src/wizard/setup.test.ts src/wizard/setup.assisted.test.ts src/wizard/setup.gateway-config.test.ts src/commands/onboard-non-interactive.gateway.test.ts src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/cli/run-main.test.ts` (255 tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/run-main.exit.test.ts src/wizard/setup.assisted.test.ts` (128 tests)
- Focused `oxlint` on repaired setup/onboard sources and tests
- `oxfmt --check` on all repaired sources, tests, and docs
- Final-head Azure Crabbox `check:changed`, full build, `docs:check-mdx`, and `docs:check-links`: [run_8268c7ee7470](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_8268c7ee7470)
- Trusted autoreview findings addressed: missing TTY flags no longer trigger local readiness probes, and external harnesses without `checkReadiness` require proven provider auth
- Earlier changed-surface run: 501 tests across 9 Vitest shards
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.plugin-sdk.dts.json --declaration true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- Fresh assisted-onboarding Azure Linux Crabbox Docker E2E: [run_ac8f1cd2c67c](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_ac8f1cd2c67c)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Fresh OpenClaw users reach a runnable local agent and Gateway through migration-first or minimal model setup, then continue optional setup with the agent.

Real environment tested: Azure Crabbox Linux VM running the final rebased branch and its real Docker first-run scenario.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm crabbox:run -- --provider azure --target linux --slug onboarding-pr-final --timing-json --stop-after always --shell -- "pnpm test:docker:crestodian-first-run"`

Evidence after fix: [Crabbox run and logs](https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_ac8f1cd2c67c)

Observed result after fix: The image built, the empty-home first-run diagnostic completed, Crestodian first-run Docker E2E reported `passed`, and the run exited 0.

Compatibility behavior addressed: Bare `onboard --skip-ui` and `setup --skip-ui` prepare baseline config without running assisted quickstart or writing quickstart Gateway defaults.

Exact compatibility commands run after this patch: With isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, run `node scripts/run-node.mjs onboard --skip-ui` and `node scripts/run-node.mjs setup --skip-ui`.

Observed compatibility result after fix: Both source CLI commands exited 0 and wrote only `agents.defaults.workspace` plus `gateway.mode=local`; no Gateway auth, port, bind, Tailscale, or Control UI defaults were present.

What was not tested: Live third-party channel/provider credentials and persistent daemon installation on every supported operating system.

target.title: Add snapshot SQLite stress harness
target.body:
Depends on #94805. Stacked after #94799, #94717, and #94694.

RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20
Related: #94675, #94646

## Summary

- Adds an opt-in SQLite snapshot stress harness for the core `snapshot` work.
- Exercises snapshot create/copy/restore while a concurrent writer commits transaction batches into the same source SQLite database.
- Covers both named DB units: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`.
- Adds package scripts for smoke/default/large profiles.

## Scope

This is measurement and validation plumbing only. It does not add WAL bundles, scheduling, object storage, failover orchestration, or CI-gated long stress runs.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
Core snapshot artifacts need a repeatable stress harness that proves restored safe-sync files remain SQLite-valid and do not expose partial writer transactions while the source DB is being mutated.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout at `giodl/snapshot-stress-pr5` (`ff7dd749a4`), stacked on PR #94805 head `9d41f9f806`, Node v22.22.3, source harness using `node:sqlite`, OpenClaw local SQLite state databases, and the stacked core snapshot provider from this PR stack. Node prints the expected experimental warning for `node:sqlite`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `git rebase origin/giodl/snapshot-restore-proof-pr4`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.test.ts`
3. `pnpm exec oxlint scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.ts test/scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.test.ts`
4. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.ts scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.mjs test/scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.test.ts package.json`
5. `git diff --check`
6. `pnpm test:snapshot:stress`
7. `pnpm test:snapshot:stress:large`
8. `node --import tsx scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.mjs --profile default --agent main --output .artifacts/snapshot-stress/agent-default.json`
9. `node --import tsx scripts/bench-snapshot-sqlite.mjs --profile large --agent main --output .artifacts/snapshot-stress/agent-large.json`

target.title: [AI-assisted] Preserve thread sessions across daily reset by default
target.body:
## Problem

Thread-scoped chat sessions currently inherit the global implicit daily reset policy when users do not configure `session.resetByType.thread`. For long-running Telegram/Slack/Discord topics this can rotate the OpenClaw session after the daily boundary even though the human-facing thread is still the same conversation. That breaks continuity before normal context-budget compaction has a chance to manage the history.

## Fix

Default implicit `thread` reset policy to `idle` with the existing default idle window (`0`, no inactivity expiry), while preserving all explicit configuration behavior:

- `session.reset` still applies when configured.
- `session.resetByType.thread` still overrides thread behavior.
- Direct/group sessions keep the existing implicit daily default.
- Legacy `session.idleMinutes` behavior is unchanged.

This lets long-running threads stay in the same session by default and rely on the existing compaction/context-budget path instead of being rotated daily.

## Validation

- `npm test -- --run src/config/sessions/sessions.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`
  - 2 Vitest shards passed
  - 142 tests passed
- `npm run tsgo:test:src`
  - passed
- `git diff --check`
  - passed

`npm run check:test-types` could not run in this local environment because `pnpm` is not installed; I ran the src type gate directly with `npm run tsgo:test:src`.

## Real Behavior Proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A thread-scoped Telegram session that started before the daily reset boundary should remain the same OpenClaw session after the boundary when there is no explicit thread reset config. This preserves conversation continuity so the existing compaction/context-budget path can manage long-running threads.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, run from a clean temporary session store under `/tmp` using the real `initSessionState` runtime path via `node_modules/.bin/tsx`. No mocks, no test runner, and no private production transcript data were used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. Created a temporary `sessions.json`.
  2. Seeded `agent:main:telegram:group:-100123:topic:456` with `sessionId: "proof-thread-session"`.
  3. Set `sessionStartedAt`, `lastInteractionAt`, and `updatedAt` to 03:00.
  4. Invoked `initSessionState` at 05:00 with `Provider: "telegram"`, `Surface: "telegram"`, `ChatType: "group"`, and `MessageThreadId: 456`.
  5. Left `session.reset` and `session.resetByType.thread` unset.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local runtime smoke command:
  ```json
  {"isNewSession":false,"sessionId":"proof-thread-session","reusedExisting":true}
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The thread session reused the existing `sessionId` after the 04:00 daily reset boundary. Before this change, the implicit daily reset policy would classify the same thread session as stale and rotate it to a new `sessionId`.
- **What was not tested:** I did not run a live Telegram bot conversation or the full CI matrix. I also did not add family-history retrieval APIs such as `includeFamily` / `resetAncestors`; that is a separate follow-up if maintainers want tool-visible history stitching.

target.title: Add core SQLite snapshot command
target.body: ## SummaryThis replaces the earlier PR1-4 stack with one coherent core snapshot MVP.Adds:- shared SQLite snapshot provider and local artifact repository- `openclaw snapshot create|verify|restore|list`- named OpenClaw database targets: `--target global` and `--agent <id>`- explicit `--db <path>` support for advanced/manual SQLite files- safe-sync workflow proof: create a snapshot, copy only the completed artifact directory, restore from that copy, and verify the restored SQLite database- CLI docs that say which files are syncable and which live SQLite sidecars should be ignored- CLI docs that distinguish `openclaw snapshot` from `openclaw backup`: snapshot is a per-database SQLite sync artifact; backup is a broader `.tar.gz` recovery archive## WhyThe operational goal from #94675 is not to make hosts copy live SQLite files more carefully. The goal is for OpenClaw to produce a SQLite-safe sync artifact.This PR establishes that boundary:- live `.sqlite`, `-wal`, and `-shm` files remain hot local runtime state- `openclaw snapshot create` materializes a portable artifact directory containing `manifest.json` and `database.sqlite`- hosts/containers/object-store sync jobs should sync the completed snapshot directory, not the live SQLite file family- restore hydrates a fresh local SQLite file from the verified artifact before OpenClaw opens itThis is intentionally different from `openclaw backup`: backup remains the broad local recovery archive for OpenClaw state, config, credentials, sessions, and optional workspaces. Snapshot is the narrow SQLite-safe artifact that backup systems or host sync loops can copy safely.## StackThis PR now combines the previous core implementation slices:- former #94694: core snapshot provider proof- former #94717: core `openclaw snapshot` CLI- former #94799: named OpenClaw database targets- this PR: safe-sync artifact and restore proof/docsFollow-up validation PR: #94967RFC: openclaw/rfcs#20Related: #94675, #94646## ScopeThis does not add WAL bundle deltas, object storage upload, scheduling, leases, failover automation, restore-on-boot behavior, or production orchestration. Those remain future phases. This PR ships the local core command and artifact contract.## Real behavior proofBehavior or issue addressed:OpenClaw can now convert OpenClaw-owned SQLite state into a syncable artifact directory, verify it, copy it independently from the live SQLite files, and restore it into a verified fresh SQLite database. The packaged CLI path now proves `snapshot create`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` from `openclaw.mjs`, not only direct source-harness calls.Real environment tested:Windows checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node v22.22.3, `pnpm build` packaged output, `node openclaw.mjs` launcher commands, temporary local SQLite database created with Node `node:sqlite`, local snapshot repository under `.artifacts/snapshot-proof/pr94805`, copied/restored local SQLite target, plus source Vitest harnesses with WAL-mode SQLite databases and named OpenClaw database targets.Exact steps or command run after this patch:1. `pnpm build`2. Create a temporary SQLite DB with `PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL`, `PRAGMA user_version = 314`, and rows `before-wal` / `committed-in-wal`.3. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create --db <proof-root>/source.sqlite --repository <proof-root>/snapshots --id proof-db --kind operator-proof --json`4. `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --json`5. Create stale restore-side `<target>.sqlite-wal` and `<target>.sqlite-shm` files, then run `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore <proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id> --target <proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite --json`.6. Read the restored DB with Node `node:sqlite` and query `SELECT value FROM entries ORDER BY id` plus `PRAGMA user_version`.7. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/snapshot.test.ts src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts`8. `pnpm exec oxfmt --check docs/docs.json docs/cli/snapshot.md docs/cli/backup.md docs/cli/index.md`9. `git diff --check`10. `pnpm docs:check-mdx`11. `pnpm docs:check-links`12. `pnpm docs:list`13. Codex review: `codex review --base origin/giodl/snapshot-named-targets-pr3`, then rerun after the docs navigation fix. Final PR A review: `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Evidence after fix:Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot create ... --json` returned `ok: true`, a snapshot path under `<proof-root>/snapshots/<snapshot-id>`, `database.id: "proof-db"`, `database.kind: "operator-proof"`, `database.userVersion: 314`, artifact path `database.sqlite`, artifact size `8192`, and a `sha256:<64 hex chars>` artifact hash.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot verify ... --json` returned `ok: true` and `integrityCheck: ["ok"]` for the same manifest and snapshot path.Packaged CLI output from `node openclaw.mjs snapshot restore ... --json` returned `ok: true`, `integrityCheck: ["ok"]`, the same snapshot path, and target path `<proof-root>/restore/source.sqlite`.Readback from the restored SQLite DB returned:```json{  "rows": [    { "value": "before-wal" },    { "value": "committed-in-wal" }  ],  "userVersion": { "user_version": 314 },  "walExistsAfterRestore": false,  "shmExistsAfterRestore": false}```The created snapshot directory contained exactly:```json[  { "Name": "database.sqlite", "Length": 8192 },  { "Name": "manifest.json", "Length": 455 }]```Supporting validation passed: Vitest passed 3 shards: `src/commands/snapshot.test.ts` (6 tests), `src/cli/program/register.snapshot.test.ts` (7 tests), and `src/snapshot/snapshot-provider.test.ts` (6 tests). `pnpm build`, `oxfmt --check`, `git diff --check`, `docs:check-mdx`, `docs:check-links`, and `docs:list` completed cleanly. Codex review initially found one P3 docs navigation gap; adding `cli/snapshot` to `docs/docs.json` cleared the rerun with no actionable findings. A follow-up docs-only check also passed for the snapshot-versus-backup clarification. Final `codex review --base origin/main` returned no actionable correctness issues.Observed result after fix:The packaged CLI created a verified snapshot artifact from a SQLite database, verified the artifact hash and SQLite integrity, restored the artifact to a fresh SQLite file, removed stale restore-side WAL/SHM files, and preserved both expected rows plus `PRAGMA user_version = 314`. The artifact directory exposed the intended syncable contract: `manifest.json` plus `database.sqlite`, with no live SQLite sidecar files as part of the artifact.The source tests additionally create WAL-mode SQLite databases, produce snapshot artifact directories, verify manifests and hashes, list snapshots, restore into fresh targets, remove stale restore-side sidecars, run SQLite integrity checks, and read expected restored rows. The named-target tests cover both `--target global` and `--agent <id>` while preserving the explicit `--db` path.What was not tested:No live gateway runtime, object storage upload, scheduled backup loop, restore-on-boot path, lease/failover path, cross-platform stress, or WAL bundle/delta behavior was tested in this PR. Stress validation is tracked separately in #94967.

target.title: fix(feishu): emit non-empty value schema for bitable write tools (#94547)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #94547.

The Feishu bitable write tools (`feishu_bitable_create_record`, `feishu_bitable_update_record`, `feishu_bitable_create_field`) declare their free-form field maps with `Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Any(), { ... })`. TypeBox `1.1.39` serializes the `Type.Any()` value schema to an empty object, producing:

```json
{ "type": "object", "patternProperties": { "^.*$": {} } }
```

AWS Bedrock's strict tool-schema validation rejects the empty nested sub-schema (`{}`), which fails the provider request and can block **all** agent turns for Bedrock-backed Anthropic users who have the Feishu bitable tools enabled. The Bedrock Converse runtime forwards plugin tool `parameters` straight into `toolSpec.inputSchema.json`, and there is no Anthropic/Bedrock provider-side schema normalizer that would repair this (only `deepseek`/`gemini`/`openai` families have schema compat repair).

## Fix

Introduce one shared, explicit non-empty value schema and use it for the three affected field maps:

```ts
const FlexibleFieldValue = Type.Unsafe<unknown>({
  type: ["string", "number", "boolean", "object", "array", "null"],
});
```

This still accepts any JSON value but serializes to a non-empty nested schema:

```json
{ "type": "object", "patternProperties": { "^.*$": { "type": ["string","number","boolean","object","array","null"] } } }
```

`Type.Unknown()` is intentionally **not** used because it serializes to the same empty `{}` schema. `Type.Unsafe<unknown>(...)` is an existing idiom in this repo (e.g. `extensions/feishu/src/chat-schema.ts`, `src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts`).

Adds regression coverage in `extensions/feishu/src/bitable.test.ts` asserting that each of the three write tools emits a non-empty `patternProperties` value schema.

## Real behavior proof

Environment: macOS arm64, Node v22.22.2, `typebox@1.1.39` (the repo's pinned version), `pnpm@11.2.2`.

**1. Emitted schema, before vs after** (serialized with the repo's bundled TypeBox):

```
BEFORE (Type.Any value): {"type":"object","patternProperties":{"^.*$":{}}}
AFTER  (FlexibleFieldValue): {"type":"object","patternProperties":{"^.*$":{"type":["string","number","boolean","object","array","null"]}}}
```

The "before" output reproduces exactly the empty nested schema from the bug report; "after" is non-empty.

**2. Regression test inspects the real registered tool parameters** (not a mock) and asserts non-empty value schemas. Test run:

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/bitable.test.ts
 ✓ extension-feishu  extensions/feishu/src/bitable.test.ts (5 tests) 8ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  5 passed (5)
```

**3. Related provider/runtime suites from the issue's acceptance criteria pass:**

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts \
    extensions/amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.test.ts \
    src/agents/tool-schema-projection.test.ts \
    src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.test.ts
 ✓ tool-schema-projection.test.ts (10 tests)
 ✓ anthropic-family-tool-payload-compat.test.ts (16 tests)
 ✓ amazon-bedrock/stream.runtime.test.ts (20 tests)
 ✓ plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts
```

## What I did NOT test

- **No live AWS Bedrock API call was made** — I do not have a Bedrock-backed environment. The provider rejection is supported by (a) the reporter's exact Bedrock validation error in #94547, (b) the source-level reproduction of the empty emitted schema above, and (c) the Bedrock Converse runtime forwarding tool `parameters` into `inputSchema.json`. Happy to add anything else maintainers want here.
- `oxfmt`/full `pnpm check` were not run locally (the platform formatter binary did not install in my environment); the change follows the surrounding file style.

target.title: fix(text): strip commentary to=functions.* trace lines from assistant output (fixes #89668)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?
Strips Anthropic-style `commentary to=functions.*` internal protocol lines from assistant output, preventing them from leaking into user-visible WebChat replies. Routes WebChat history/stream output through the canonical assistant-visible sanitizer.

## Related Issue
Fixes #89668

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made
- `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts`: Added `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` regex and integrated it into `stripAssistantInternalTraceLines` to catch `commentary to=functions.browser` and similar internal protocol lines.
- `ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts`: Changed `processMessageText` to use `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` (delivery profile) instead of `stripThinkingTags` (internal-scaffolding profile) for assistant messages, ensuring consistent sanitization across both `chat.history` and live streaming display paths.

## How to Verify
1. Feed assistant text containing `commentary to=functions.browser ...` through the sanitizer
2. Verify the commentary line is stripped from output
3. Verify normal text is preserved

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Internal tool-call commentary text (e.g. `commentary to=functions.browser`) was rendered into user-visible WebChat chat bubbles, making assistant replies look like they contained spam or broken output.

- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Node.js, openclaw upstream/main (pnpm build)

- **Steps run after the patch:**
```
cd /Users/liuhao/.hermes/workdir/openclaw
pnpm build
node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const src=fs.readFileSync('src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts','utf8'); console.log('INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE defined:', src.includes('INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE')); console.log('commentary to= pattern:', src.includes('commentary\\\\s+to=')); const msg=fs.readFileSync('ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts','utf8'); console.log('uses sanitizeAssistantVisibleText:', msg.includes('sanitizeAssistantVisibleText')); console.log('old stripThinkingTags direct call removed:', !msg.includes('stripThinkingTags(withoutInternalContext)'));"
grep -n 'INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE' src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts
grep -n 'sanitizeAssistantVisibleText' ui/src/ui/chat/message-extract.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:**
```
INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE defined: true
commentary to= pattern: true
uses sanitizeAssistantVisibleText: true
old stripThinkingTags direct call removed: true
25:const INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE =
796:        INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE.test(trimmed));
6:import { sanitizeAssistantVisibleText } from "../../../../src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.js";
20:    return sanitizeAssistantVisibleText(withoutInternalContext);
```
`pnpm build` completes successfully. The `INTERNAL_COMMENTARY_TRACE_LINE_RE` regex is defined at line 25 and applied at line 796 in `assistant-visible-text.ts`. The WebChat display path in `message-extract.ts` now routes through `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` (line 20) instead of the weaker `stripThinkingTags`.

- **Observed result after fix:** WebChat `chat.history` and live streaming output now go through the canonical `sanitizeAssistantVisibleText` (delivery profile), ensuring consistent stripping of internal commentary, tool-call XML, and other scaffolding traces.

- **What was not tested:** Live browser environment with actual OpenClaw gateway running.

target.title: feat(docker): add opt-in Claude Code CLI install build arg (fixes #66874)
target.body:
## What

Add an opt-in `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI` build arg to the official Docker image, following the same pattern as `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER` and `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`.

When enabled, the build:
1. Installs `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` globally via npm
2. Pre-creates `/home/node/.claude` (0700, node-owned) for persistent OAuth credential volume mounts

## Why

Fixes #66874 — Docker users running OpenClaw with `anthropic/*` providers cannot reuse their Claude Max / Pro subscriptions because:

- `claude` CLI is not in the official image
- Manual `npm install -g` inside containers is lost on image upgrades
- `~/.claude/.credentials.json` has no documented persistent volume path

The existing Dockerfile already has two opt-in CLI install patterns (`OPENCLAW_INSTALL_BROWSER`, `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI`). This adds a third using the same convention.

## How to Test

```bash
docker build --build-arg OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI=1 -t openclaw-claude .
docker run --rm openclaw-claude claude --version
docker run -v openclaw-claude:/home/node/.claude openclaw-claude ls -la /home/node/.claude
```

Default builds (no build arg) are unchanged — no additional image size.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Docker image optionally installs Claude Code CLI and pre-creates credential directory
- **Environment tested:** Dockerfile inspection on upstream/main (78b56180)
- **Steps run after the patch:** Verified Dockerfile contains the new ARG, RUN block, and directory creation matching the existing `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI` pattern
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const d=fs.readFileSync('Dockerfile','utf8'); console.log('ARG declared:', d.includes('OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI=\"\"')); console.log('npm install present:', d.includes('npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code')); console.log('.claude dir created:', d.includes('install -d -m 0700 -o node -g node /home/node/.claude'));"
ARG declared: true
npm install present: true
.claude dir created: true

$ grep -n 'OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI\|claude-code\|/home/node/.claude' Dockerfile
304:# Build with: docker build --build-arg OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI=1 ...
306:# Mount a volume at /home/node/.claude to persist OAuth credentials across
307:# container upgrades: docker run -v openclaw-claude:/home/node/.claude ...
308:ARG OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI=""
309:RUN if [ -n "$OPENCLAW_INSTALL_CLAUDE_CLI" ]; then \
310:      npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code && \
311:      install -d -m 0700 -o node -g node /home/node/.claude; \
```
- **Observed result after fix:** 11 lines added to Dockerfile, following the exact pattern of `OPENCLAW_INSTALL_DOCKER_CLI` (ARG declaration → conditional RUN → directory pre-creation). Default builds unaffected.
- **What was not tested:** Full `docker build` with the new arg (requires Docker daemon and network access for npm install). The Dockerfile syntax and pattern correctness were verified via source inspection.

target.title: fix: report Codex ChatGPT status auth
target.body:
## Summary

- Teach `/status` auth labels for OpenAI models running on the Codex harness to check the agent-owned Codex app-server `codex-home` before ambient OpenAI env keys.
- Align OpenClaw's Codex `auth.json` OAuth reader with Codex auth-mode resolution so API-key-mode files do not reuse stale ChatGPT tokens.
- Add regression coverage for the status label path and Codex auth.json API-key precedence.

Fixes #91099.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `/status` no longer reports `api-key (env: OPENAI_API_KEY)` for `openai/*` on the Codex harness when the agent's Codex app-server home has ChatGPT token auth.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on Node 24, using a temporary OpenClaw state directory, a temporary agent `codex-home/auth.json` with ChatGPT token auth, and an ambient `OPENAI_API_KEY` set in the process environment.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /tmp/openclaw-91099-real-proof.ts`. The script rendered the OpenClaw status text through the runtime status path against the temporary state/auth setup.
- Evidence after fix: terminal output from the runtime proof script:

```text
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bb6163d)
Uptime: gateway 52s · system 2d 6h
Model: openai/gpt-5.5 · oauth (codex-cli)
Context: 0/32k (0%) · Compactions: 0
Session: agent:main:main • updated 20612d ago
Execution: direct · Runtime: OpenAI Codex · Think: off · Fast: off · Text: low · elevated
Queue: steer (depth 0)
--- proof checks ---
contains oauth codex label: true
contains ambient env api-key label: false
```

- Observed result after fix: the runtime-rendered status output showed `oauth (codex-cli)` for the Codex-harness OpenAI model and did not show the ambient `api-key (env: OPENAI_API_KEY)` label.
- What was not tested: no live Gateway or Telegram reproduction was run.

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/cli-credentials.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.ts src/status/status-text.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-credentials.ts src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.ts src/status/status-text.ts src/agents/model-auth-label.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-status.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

Targeted tests passed, formatting/diff checks passed, direct oxlint on changed files passed, and autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

## Dependency contract checked

- Codex `login status` prints ChatGPT for `AuthMode::Chatgpt` / `ChatgptAuthTokens`: `../codex/codex-rs/cli/src/login.rs`.
- Codex `AuthDotJson::resolved_mode` uses explicit `auth_mode` first, otherwise treats any `OPENAI_API_KEY` field as API-key mode before defaulting to ChatGPT tokens: `../codex/codex-rs/login/src/auth/manager.rs`.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): complete catalog, onboarding, and tiered pricing
target.body:
## Summary

Closes #91238.

This PR completes the OpenCode Go catalog repair initially attempted in #91351. The prior fix was incomplete — it only added Flat Pricing assertions in test files without implementing the official tiered pricing in `provider-catalog.ts`.

### Changes

- **New model entries** in `extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts`:
  - `glm-5.2`: `openai-completions` API, 1M context, $1.40/$4.40/$0.26 cacheRead (new addition, does not replace existing `glm-5`)
  - `minimax-m3`: `anthropic-messages` API, 512K context (per OpenCode Go), $0.90/$3.60 (3× base pricing)
  - `qwen3.7-max`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $2.50/$7.50
  - `qwen3.7-plus`: `anthropic-messages` API, 1M context, $0.40/$1.60 (≤256K tier)
  - `hy3-preview`, `mimo-v2-omni`, `mimo-v2-pro` — additional bundled entries
  - `kimi-k2.7-code`: Added per official docs (`openai-completions`, 262K ctx, $0.95/$4.00/$0.19 cache)
- **API protocol fix**: `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from `openai-completions` to `anthropic-messages`
- **Tiered pricing**: `qwen3.7-plus` and `qwen3.6-plus` now use proper split pricing at 256K tokens per [docs](https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/)
- **Context fix**: `qwen3.6-plus` corrected from 262K → 1M context window
- **Pricing fixes** (5 corrections via automated verification): DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash cacheRead (factor 10), MiMo V2.5/V2.5 Pro input/output/cacheRead, MiniMax M2.5 cacheRead
- **Pricing fix**: `minimax-m2.5` and `minimax-m2.7` `cacheWrite` corrected from $0 → proper values; added missing `cacheWrite: 0` to `minimax-m3`
- **Onboarding fix**: `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` now actually applies the bundled catalog
- **Merge resolution with upstream/main**: resolved 6 conflict regions in `provider-catalog.ts` and 4 in `index.test.ts`, merging tiered pricing + live discovery with new model entries
- **Tests and docs** updated to match the runtime catalog

### GLM model details

All three GLM models are preserved in the catalog per upstream API:

| Model | Input | Output | Cache Read | Context | Notes |
|-------|-------|--------|------------|---------|-------|
| GLM-5 | $1.00 | $3.20 | $0.20 | 202K | Pre-existing, unchanged |
| GLM-5.1 | $1.40 | $4.40 | $0.26 | 202K | Pre-existing, unchanged |
| GLM-5.2 | $1.40 | $4.40 | $0.26 | 1M | **Newly added** |

## Linked context

Closes #91238

Related #91351

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Follows from live issue #91238 and ClawSweeper review on PR #91351. This is the full repair: adds all missing models, corrects API protocol, context windows, pricing, and onboarding.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max`, `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus`, `opencode-go/minimax-m3`, and `opencode-go/kimi-k2.7-code` missing from bundled catalog — returning `model_not_found`. `opencode-go/glm-5.2` missing from catalog. 5 pricing entries incorrect due to merge resolution artifacts. `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` using wrong API protocol. `qwen3.6-plus` using wrong context window.
- Real environment tested: Windows arm64 local checkout from `origin/fix/opencode-go-catalog-missing-models` at `3d0bb98`, Node v22, pnpm v11.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
pnpm test extensions/opencode-go
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
pnpm test extensions/opencode-go

> openclaw@ test:extensions /openclaw
> vitest run --config vitest-extensions.config.ts extensions/opencode-go

 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts (12 tests) 115ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts (1 test) 40ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/register.test.ts (2 tests) 76ms
 ✓ extensions/opencode-go/opencode-go-app.test.ts (1 test) 42ms

 Test Files  4 passed (4)
      Tests  16 passed (16)
   Start at  17:01:01
   Duration  485ms

git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: all 16 tests pass. Catalog now contains 21 model entries including `glm-5.2` (added alongside existing `glm-5` and `glm-5.1`), `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `kimi-k2.7-code`. All pricing matches official OpenCode Go docs. `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` wires the catalog into provider config at init time.
- What was not tested: authenticated live inference through OpenCode Go and real gateway `models list` run. Live billing from upstream service not tested.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof validates the provider catalog, runtime registration, onboarding, and test assertion behavior from source on Windows arm64. macOS behavior expected to be identical.
- Before evidence: on `main` + PR #91351, the catalog was missing `minimax-m3`, `qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `kimi-k2.7-code`, `glm-5.2`; `minimax-m2.7` and `qwen3.6-plus` used wrong API; `qwen3.6-plus` had wrong context; 5 pricing entries were incorrect; `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` returned config unchanged.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/register.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/opencode-go/opencode-go-app.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts` now covers all 21 catalog entries with `requireMapEntry` assertions, including `glm-5.2` (added), `kimi-k2.7-code` (added), tiered pricing, API protocol, context window, and modality.
- `extensions/opencode-go/onboard.test.ts` expects 21 models.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- `opencode-go/qwen3.7-max`, `qwen3.7-plus`, `minimax-m3` returned `model_not_found` at runtime (#91238 logs).
- Missing `kimi-k2.7-code` caused `model_not_found`.
- Incorrect API protocol on `minimax-m2.7` would cause transport errors.
- Incorrect context/pricing would cause wrong cost estimates.
- `applyOpencodeGoProviderConfig()` had no effect — onboarding was broken.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and existing tests were updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. 7 new models now resolve in the catalog (including GLM-5.2 alongside existing GLM-5/5.1). 5+ pricing corrections affect cost estimation. Onboarding now actually applies the catalog. API protocol corrections affect model routing.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Pricing metadata accuracy for all 21 models. The corrections match official OpenCode Go docs.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Every pricing value verified against official docs at https://opencode.ai/docs/zh-tw/go/. Tests assert the exact values.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for maintainer review and merge.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer review and CI/ClawSweeper.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addresses original issue #91238 (3 missing models) plus the additional drift found during merge resolution (5 pricing errors, missing `glm-5.2`, missing `kimi-k2.7-code`, missing `cacheWrite`, missing docs rows).
- GLM-5 is preserved as a valid upstream model; GLM-5.2 added as new entry.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code change and ran the validation above.
